LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN MATEO COUNTY TRANSIT DISTRICT DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. FAILURE TO PLEAD FACTS DEMONSTRATING OR EXCUSING COMPLIANCE WITH THE CLAIM PRESENTATION REQUIREMENT MAY BE RAISED ON A GENERAL DEMURRER FOR FAILURE TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION. STATE VS. SUPERIOR COURT (2004) 32 CAL.4TH 1234. AMBIGUITY CREATED BY CHECKING BOTH BOXES UNDER SECTION 9 OF THE JUDICIAL COUNSEL FORM COMPLAINT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SAN MATEO COUNTY TRANSIT DISTRICT DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Failure to plead facts demonstrating or excusing compliance with the claim presentation requirement may be raised on a general demurrer for failure to state a cause of action. State vs. Superior Court (2004) 32 Cal.4th 1234. Ambiguity created by checking both boxes under Section 9 of the Judicial Counsel Form Complaint. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.)
